data.name: Aachen
data.address:
Augustinerbach 2a
52056 Aachen
Germany
data.description: We are one of the elder Local Committees and one of the foundation members of EESTEC, in 1986. Our members are students of Electrical Engineering and Information Technology at RWTH Aachen University and University of Applied Sciences Aachen. We have a close cooperation with the EE Student Council of RWTH Aachen, especially concerning excursions. Regularly, we organize the EESTech Challenge Local Round Aachen in summer and the Aixtec Workshop in winter.

data.name: Ankara
data.address:
Middle East Technical University
Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering\A-203 06531 Ankara
Turkey
data.description: EESTEC LC Ankara is founded in 2006 as the ﬁrst Local Committee of EESTEC in Asia. Even though it is a relatively young LC, with more than 250 members and successful events, EESTEC LC Ankara is one of the most successful local committees through the Europe. In 14 years, EESTEC LC Ankara hosted over 300 European students for 18 international events and more than 360 students from Ankara are given the opportunity to attend international workshops. Furthermore, local events in different scopes are started to be organized by LC Ankara, in order to present career, education, soft skills and development opportunities to its members.

data.name: Athens
data.address: Iroon Politechniou 9, Zografou, Greece
data.description: LC Athens was founded in 2004 by a handful of students in the National Technical University of Athens. Athens became ofﬁcially an EESTEC Local Committee in Congress 2005 in Madrid. During these ten years, more than 900 members have joined our local committee, and the number is still increasing. At this point, we have around 90 active members, willing to help our LC make a step further. Having organized nine successful international workshops, with more than 460 participants from all over Europe, LC Athens deserves the title of "must go" EESTEC LC. We are, also, very active in our faculty, organizing many interesting local activities, not only for our members, but also for the rest of the students. In this way, a lot of students gain interest in EESTEC, and we keep on spreading around the"EESTEC Spirit"!

data.name: Banja Luka
data.address:
Patre 5th
Faculty of Electrical Engineering
78 000 Banja Luka
Bosnia
data.description: Local committee of Banja Luka has been accepted in a full membership on EESTEC congress in Belgrade, in April 2004. In June 2004 Local committees registered in Banja Luka court as a non-profit, student’s organization with a headquarters on Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Patre 5 street. Year in year out, many activities are occurring, with the goal of popularizing student volunteering and gather more members. By participating in the Local committee, students of electrical engineering are gathering organizational skills which are surely important in later professional career. Through those years we helped over 200 students of Electrical engineering in Banja Luka to participate in some of the EESTEC events. Significant fact is that, besides opportunity for professional development, considering that students have 53 different destinations to visit, by participating in workshop, students are able to represent our culture, our customs and break possible prejudices. In April 2012, Local committee organized first Soft Skills Academy for 30 students of Banja Luka University. Students were able to work on development of their personal and professional skills necessary for purchasing successful career in their field. Having got great feedback considering our first Soft Skills Academy, in 2013 and 2014 we decided to organize it again because apparently, students find it very useful and interesting. EESTEC Banja Luka’s SSA is slowly becoming a tradition, and our LC is getting recognized as one of the most important student organisation of the city.

data.name: Belgrade
data.address:
Bulevar kralja Aleksandra 73
11000 Belgrade
Serbia
data.description: LC Belgrade became an official Member of EESTEC at the Congress in London in 2000, and nowadays is one of the largest and the best organized Local Committees in Europe. Throughout 22 years of our existence, over 1000 students of the School of Electrical Engineering in Belgrade have participated in numerous workshops, exchanges and professional seminars in cities across Europe.Since the establishment of our LC, we have organized over 100 local and international projects that have been encouraging personal and professional development of EECS students. With more than 350 members and more than 5 annual projects, we strive to create opportunities for the future development of our students and our Association. How do we do it? We organize numerous local projects for the students of our University, such as "JobFair - Kreiraj svoju budućnost" that has been annually organized for the last 18 years, with the aim to prepare young people for their future career by providing various opportunities such as internships, employment and the direct contact with over 63 companies. Another significant project of ours is “Brand New Engineers - Od integrala do inženjera”, that strives to provide the academical knowledge and insights in popular Engineering topics, with the help of our Faculty’s professors. Every year the project encompasses at least one hackathon by a sponsoring company. We are especially proud of our project “Soft Skills Academy” which has been organized every year since 2010 and was later taken as an International project of EESTEC.Since 2016, we have taken part in organizing the regional project PLC+ SIM(P)ATIC Challenge, together with LC Zagreb and LC Ljubljana. When it comes to international events, we have recently organized the anniversary 30th Congress in 2016, which was followed by the 1st Autumn Congress in the same year. In May 2017, we organized the third edition of exchange "Belgrade State of Mind". In December 2018, we have organise a long avaited workshop, Belgrade Spy Agency. April of 2019 was marked by the first edition of the operational event Brand.Comm, that was created by LC Belgrade.Everything we do is with belief in outstanding individuals and with the aim to motivate students to work on their self-improvement and discovering, thus contributing to the University community in our country. We are proud to be a part of this Association that provides young people with a new perspective in life, technology, engineering, communications, travel, new experiences, new friends and successful projects. What we get through volunteering in our Local Committee are professional skills, discovering ourselves and others through local and international projects, and as the greatest gift of all, the inevitable happiness of having a great number of outstanding individuals as friends in the whole Europe.

data.name: Bucharest
data.address:
Splaiul Independentei nr.313
corp Rectorat
AN 204bis
060042 Bucharest
Romania
data.description: Greetings from Bucharest! We have been part of EESTEC’s family for several years, precisely since the Congress in Cosenza, which was organized between the 9th and the 16th of April 2003. EESTEC LC Bucharest activates in Politehnica University of Bucharest, thus placing itself in the ideal environment for accomplishing its academic goals.Our mission is to help students build a better future. In order for us to do that, we organize local workshops and conferences, as well as international workshops and exchanges. The events are based on personal and professional development. Our workshops are centered on specific subjects: electrical engineering, information technology, renewable energies, computer programming and embedded technologies. Also, we organize motivational weekends for our guests, where we just have a good time and get to know each other better.We hosted 2 exchanges so far: ROSummer Spirit (in the summer of 2009) and EUROtrip (in the summer of 2015). Our latest international workshop was organised in May 2016 and it was called Embedded Minds.LC Bucharest is very active locally, ranging from contests and soft skills trainings to conferences and charitable parties. The most popular are: EESTEC Olympics(8 editions), Soft Skills Academy(4 editions) and T4T(9 editions). Last but not least, each year we organize ICE (Inner Circle of EESTEC), which is a 3 days training, designed for new members.Stay tuned for the “What?”, “When?” and “Where?” of our future events ;)

data.name: Eskisehir
data.address: Anadolu University, Yunus Emre Campus, Student Center Floor:3 No:308 Tepebasi/Eskişehir/Turkey
data.description:
EESTEC LC Eskisehir, which was founded in 2006, was the 3rd local committee in Turkey. Its members are mostly from Eskisehir Technical University, Faculty of Engineering, but also it has members from the different faculties of Eskisehir Technical, Eskisehir Osmangazi and Anadolu University. 

Most notable events of LC Eskisehir are 6th ECM, What Does The Python Say? , Regional Meeting: Ten Shades of Glory, 8th Design Sprint, Live Regional Meeting for Region I: Return Of The Dragons and 7th Brand.Comm

data.name: Guimarães
data.description: EESTEC Observer Guimarães was founded in April 2019 in the northern region of Portugal. Being a recent group, it already has 16 members.We are based in the city of Guimarães where we can find the Guimarães castle, monument where the first king of Portugal, D. Afonso Henriques was born and where it took place here an important battle that staged the course in history for our country because it granted us a certain "independence" from the kingdom of Leon. We hope to contribute and learn as many as we can from the rest of the members, share what our country has to give, our roots and traditions and open our horizons to new cultures and realities.Best regards,Observer Guimarães.

data.name: Istanbul
data.address:
EESTEC LC Istanbul
ITÜ Elektrik-Elektronik Fakültesi
Maslak\Istanbul
34469
Turkey
data.description: EESTEC LC Istanbul is located at the Faculty of Electrical and Electronics, for computer science students, electrical, electronic, control, and telecommunications engineering students, in order to take advantage of the opportunities offered by EESTEC.        LC Istanbul is located in Istanbul Technical University. Students of our faculty created EESTEC Istanbul Local Committee, in February 2005. Currently, EESTEC LC Istanbul participates in international events and organizes workshops and operational events in Istanbul. EESTEC LC Istanbul was the ﬁrst local committee in Turkey, as it had the responsibility of promoting it all over Turkey and establishing new commitments; as a result, the commitments were established in Ankara and Eskisehir.     EESTEC LC Istanbul’s goal is to participate in international seminars and exchange programs, share technical informations, social and cultural values and to introduce other countries and the education system to students. In addition, EESTEC LC Istanbul also makes international career, job and internship opportunities possible. Besides, LC Istanbul helps its active members, to gain organizational skills, to establish closer relations with organizations operating in the sector, to share social and cultural values and sharing opportunities, and promotion of Istanbul with local events in Istanbul.#lcistanbulrocks

data.name: Izmir
data.address:
Dokuz Eylül Üniversitesi, Tinaztepe Kampüsü, Elektrik-Elektronik Müh., Toplu-luklar odasi
Buca/Izmir
Turkey
data.description: LC  Izmir is one of the four Local Committees in Turkey. Our ﬁrst contact with EESTEC is dated back to 2009. Our ﬁrst workshop was held between 5th and 11th of September, 2011. Later, we got Local Committee status. There are nine universities in Izmir,  four of which our members study.

data.name: Karlsruhe
data.address: EESTEC Karlsruhe e.V., c/o AStA am KIT, Adenauerring 7, DE - 76131 Karlsruhe
data.description: EESTEC LC Karlsruhe is a group of highly motivated students, always ready to make something special in Karlsruhe. As of June 2019, we number around 15 active members. We have already done two international workshops "Electrify: Mobility of Tomorrow" and "Stayin' alive", both with their respective IMWs. Both events were huge successes and we were able to show our beautiful city to you EESTECers!Locally, we are organising your yearly Skill Academy, providing local students from our university with both soft and hard skill workshops.Stay tuned which international event is coming next!The city of Karlsruhe is the second-largest city in the state of Baden-Württemberg, in southwest Germany, near the Franco-German border. The city is one of the warmest and sunniest in Germany. It was planned with the palace tower (Schloss) at the center and 32 streets radiating out from it like the spokes of a wheel, or the ribs of a folding fan, so that one nickname for Karlsruhe in German is the "fan city" (Fächerstadt). Our group is seated at the Karlsruhe Institute of Technology (KIT). KIT is one of the largest and most prestigious research and education institutions in Germany known for the high quality of research work around the world. In the 2011 performance ranking of scientific papers, Karlsruhe ranked first in Germany and among the top ten universities in Europe in engineering and natural sciences. Karl Benz studied mechanical engineering at this University and it was here that Heinrich Hertz proved the existence of electromagnetic waves in the late 1880s.Like our Facebook page and invite your fellow EESTECers and don't forget to visit our website!!!

data.name: Krakow
data.address:
Akademia Gorniczo-Hutnicza im. Stanislawa Staszica w Krakowie
Al. Mickiewicza 30
30-059 Krakow
data.description:
LC Krakow, the first Local Committee of EESTEC in Poland, is a student organization based at the AGH University in Kraków. We work closely with the Faculty of Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Automatics and Biomedical Engineering, and Electronics and Telecommunications.
The involvement of Kraków students in EESTEC dates back to 1986 when a delegation from Poland, including representatives from AGH, helped found the organization. However, LC Krakow was officially established in 1998. Initially part of the Academic Circle of Electronics, we became an independent student organization in 2007 and were formally registered with EESTEC in 2009.
Today, our committee boasts over 100 active members. We provide opportunities for developing soft skills and participating in various training sessions. EESTEC is primarily about people - for 28 years, our committee has been fostering lifelong friendships and connections. Our goal is not only to create a great community but also to give back to society. We organize various external events, with our flagship events including Hacknarok, SSA, and many more, reaching thousands of AGH students.

data.name: Lille
data.address:
Club EESTEC
Polytech Lille, B111
Avenue Paul Langevin
59655 Villeneuve d'Ascq cedex
France
data.description: HistoryCreated in 2008Junior Local Committee in Congress 2010            Workshop : Robotics and Intelligent Systems in 2012Local Committee in Congress 2012            Workshop : ElectROniCKs in 2014            Workshop : SILilleCON VALLEY in 2016            Workshop : SILilleCON Valley 2.0 in 2018About Us We're a students association in our engineering school Polytech Lille, located in the campus of the Technological University of Lille.Proud to be the only one left in France, we're excited to meet you and make you discover our city !With around 8 members this year, and after some much needed re-ornganaization we stand stronger and ready to make you come to us !We're also delighted to see more and more members going to your events !See you soon

data.name: Ljubljana
data.address:
EESTEC, društvo študentov elektrotehnike in računalništva
 ŠOU - mednarodna pisarna
 Pivovarniška ulica 6
1000 Ljubljana
Slovenia
data.description: LC Ljubljana, one of the founding LCs of EESTEC, has been active for more than 30 years, and since then our members have been setting high standards with their enthusiasm and ingenuity.Our home institution is the University of Ljubljana, with our members studying at the Faculty of Electrical Engineering and the Faculty of Computer and Information Science. In our local environment, we organize different lectures, soft skills trainings, promote interesting events. The biggest and most important annual local project is JobFair, which we first organized in 2007. It's an event where companies can meet their potential employees and students can take their first steps of building a career. The fair consists of booth presentations of numerous companies as well as interesting lectures, moderated discussions and speed dating with employers. With such actions, we try to help students get the most out of their study years.On the international level, we regularly organize workshops for foreign students, which traditionally conclude with one of our legendary International Motivational Weekends situated in our beautiful countryside. We also have experience with handling bigger events: we organized the 1987 and 1999 Congress and jointly with LC Rijeka the 2008 Congress. In 2014 we prepared our first Advanced Skills Workshop. We always welcome our international guests with awesome parties and one of the most desirable drinks in EESTEC - Borovničke.We have big plans for the future, as we never run out of highly motivated members, eager to try something new, improve their skills and of course spread EESTEC Spirit!

data.name: Madrid
data.address:
Eurielec - EESTEC LC Madrid
ETSI Telecomunicación UPM
Avda.Complutense 30
28040 - Madrid
 Spain
data.description: Hola everyone! LC Madrid is one of the committees which founded EESTEC back in the 80s, being a former member of the old association which lead to the formation of EESTEC: Eurielec. Since we are very lazy, we have kept this old name, Eurielec, for decades, and it has become now part of our identity. We are located at the Telecommunication School of the Technical University of Madrid (ETSIT UPM), and so far, we are the only LC in Spain. At local level, we work on different Electrical Engineering and Computer Science projects, such as the organisation of Linux and open software courses or the organisation of small workshops in electronics projects. We also work in projects about soft skills, working and travelling in Europe. Recently, we have tended to be very involved in EESTEC at international level, from bodies to BAs and even the Board. If you want to meet us, don’t hesitate to apply for one of our events!Picture from: Cristina Pérez Chocano, Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY-SA 3.0.

data.name: Milan
data.description: Our commitment was born in 2016 and, since then, our ride and our aim to grow never stopped, thanks to some motivated students of the Politecnico di Milano. Every year we grow up in numbers and experience and many of our members took part in International teams.We try to be something different from the daily routine, a place where every student can feel at home, learn new things, work with other people and travel all around Europe, breaking away from the usual perspective through which University is looked at and believing that all of us members are friends before associates.We successfully hosted local rounds of the EESTech Challenge in 2018 and 2019. In October 2018 we had our first IMW, named “Tacà Foeugh!”: we went to Monferrato, the UNESCO heritage Piedmont countryside, to visit castles, taste some good wine and some typical Italian food.We are also glad to say that in two month we are hosting our first international workshop in Milan, about Internet of Things (IoT)!Throughout the whole life of the association, we also organized local initiatives and workshops held by members and professors as to spread the EESTEC Spirit in our university: we organized an event about recognizing obstacles and moving in the environment with Arduino, a course about Python and a series of conferences about StartUps.We are also ready to start 2 new projects: the first one named Arcade-my, that consists in a set of four lectures about video games design. The second one consists in some training about soft skills, to improve and enhance personal potentials.Our current aim is to grow bigger both locally and internationally and to introduce all the students to our restless and highly enthusiastic way of facing life.

data.name: Munich
data.address:
EESTEC Munich e.V.
Technische Universität München
Arcisstr.21
80333 München
Germany
data.description: LC Munich, also known as LC Awesome, is a very active committee in EESTEC.  Since its foundation in 1999 many workshops were organised. In 2013 & 2023 we were the organising committee of EESTEC’s greatest event: the congress. Beside the workshops, we organize an exchange, motivational weekends and a job fair every year. LC Munich organizes at least one workshop per year since 2005. In the course of this, 15-20 students of electrical engineering from all over Europe are invited to Munich for one week. During the workshop, both participants and local organizers get the opportunity to share their experiences and ideas. As a result, the students get to widen their technical and intercultural competences, as much as their cultural horizon. Of course that week also challenges in a linguistic matter and therefore can be used to improve one's English skills. One of the key components of a workshop are its seminars, which typically take place during 3-5 days of the stay.So far, LC Munich has organized workshops about topics concerning Plone, micro, nanoelectronics, robotics, "Intercultural competences" in cooperation with the Carl von Linde-Akademie of the TUM and "Women in Engineering" in cooperation with the Diversity Center of the TUM.Although our previous seminars always were in collaboration with a chair of the TU Munich, we would be very interested in working together with companies as well. In May 2015 we organized a double-event with the workshop ‘Robotronics’ and the T.O.O.L. ‘Leaders of Tomorrow 2’. Something everyone associates with Munich is the world's largest folk festival: the Oktoberfest. To give EESTECers the opportuniy to join this great event, we organize an exchange during this time every year. As the hostel prices are ridiculous during the time of Oktoberfest, we host the participants privately. Until 2015 we were able to organize the Oktoberfest Exchange five times. Motivational weekends take place from time to time, in order to motivate new and old members for EESTEC and to give anyone interested the opportunity to get to know EESTEC. Mostly it includes two softskill-seminars, community games, parties and hiking. Either these weekends take place as a cooperation with local committees from other cities and countries, in which case they are called International Motivational Weekends, or they are just organized for local students, together with the participants of a recent workshop. Since the OpenDay during the congress in 2013, we decided to keep up the contacts and to organize the job fair carEEr every year to give students the opportunity to get in touch with companies. Until 2019 we were able to organise it six times.

data.name: Nis
data.address:
Aleksandra Medvedeva 14
18000 Niš
Serbia
data.description:
Currently, EESTEC Local Committee Nis counts about 30 active members who are totally committed to maintain the EESTEC atmosphere at the Faculty of Electronic Engineering in Niš, as well as the city in general. We, in the Local Committee Nis, believe that being a good engineer does not only involve possessing technical knowledge, but rather good social skills as well. In light of that, we devote a lot of attention to that kind of knowledge. Moreover, we know that acquiring knowledge is nothing without having fun! That’s why we try to combine both components equally during our events. In last 12 months we organized 2 international events: "Smart technologies’ power for your future" (5th edition in February 2017) and "Jazz do it 2017"  (6th edition in August 2017), but also two big local events: "CodeCraft 2017" (2nd edition in October 2017) and "IEEESTEC Student Conference"(10th edition in November 2017). In this way we are trying to raise academical level in EESTEC. We are looking forward to continuing all these projects and we would be honored if you participate in our future events! Few years ago we had generation change in our LC, now we gathered enough new members, we raised our motivation, so we are ready for new challenges, new tasks and new successful projects! 
Your EESTEC LC Nis

data.name: Novi Sad
data.address:
Trg Dositeja Obradovica 6
21000 Novi Sad
Serbia
data.description:
EESTEC LC Novi Sad is a local committee established in 2001 at the conference in London. 
Our association brings together students from the Faculty of Technical Sciences in Novi Sad with the Department of Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, and Mechatronics. Since membership in EESTEC carries the responsibility and obligation, not just membership "on paper", EESTEC LC Novi Sad, with over 250 members, is one of the largest and most active local committees. We organized 14 exchanges and 7 professional seminars so far and hosted more than 200 colleagues from European technical universities, while nearly 260 of our students are sent on the road to Europe. 
EESTEC LC Novi Sad in 2010. received a great honor to organize the ECM (EESTEC Chairpersons’ Meeting), one of the most important events with in the organization. On the occasion of the Presidential Conference of electrical engineering students we have hosted over 70 participants who were actively working during the event to create a new development EESTEC strategy, involving numerous projects in the coming years.
EESTEC LC Novi Sad has extended the domain of their activities, and become very active at the local level. With the excellent cooperation with companies and universities, we organized four major competitions in programming, several presentations and seminars for local students and a jobfair KONTEH. In May 2020, our committee organized Spring Congress, which was held online for the first time. 
For several years, EESTEC LC Novi Sad has a full support from the University of Novi Sad, Faculty of Engineering, Provincial Department of Sport and Youth, Provincial Department of Education and Culture, and most recently by the Ministry of Youth and Sports of the Republic of Serbia.

data.name: Patras
data.description: Hey, guess who’s here? That’s right, it’s LC Patras, your favourite LC! The people who started dancing “Pes mou ti” and spread it to every EESTEC Party! Wanna know more about our branch? We are primarily students from the University of Patras. Patras is the third biggest city in Greece and you can find it in the southwest part of Greece on the upper part of the peninsula of Peloponnese. Patras is famous for its carnival and traditional drinks “mavrodafni” and “tentura”.Our branch counts more than 90 active members who are highly motivated and like to organize many events including workshops, soft skills seminars, etc. However, it wasn’t always like that. Even though Patras joined EESTEC in 2006 and became an LC quite fast, in 2012 it got downgraded back to an Observer, due to a lack of members. But since then we are growing and in 2015 we got back our LC status. In our LC our goal is to keep a balance between academic and fun activities. That’s why we not only focus on our members’ professional and personal development but we also try to create a friendly environment and bond with each other! Don’t miss a chance to visit us at our next events and experience the authentic LC Patras Spirit!

data.name: Podgorica
data.address:
University of Montenegro, Faculty of Electrical Engineering
Dzordza Vasingtona bb.
 20 000 Podgorica
Montenegro
data.description: Local Committee Podgorica was ofﬁcially accepted into full membershipon EESTEC Congress inDelft, in 2000th year and it is theonly local committee in Montenegro.It currently has about 120 members.We are aware of the seriousnessof our future calls as wellas quality of knowledge we adoptat our university, but we believethat knowledge acquired by doingsomething practical is a big plusfor future engineers, and it’s mucheasier to acquire knowledge through fun so we try to procure that to our mem-bers. Most of our members are active, and few of them are alumni members whohelped us a lot in organizing our events. We organized different kind of events tillnow. Some events were with educational character – seminars about energeticsand telecommunications, course of web programming, yearly charity events– charity action of collecting clothes, candies and school tools for children withdisabilities from a local school, summer exchanges, motivational weekends anda workshop this summer.EESTEC LC Podgorica is one of the most active student’s organization inMontenegro and has very good cooperation with similar organizations. We areplanning to organize more projects which, we hope will be very interesting for ourand students from other colleges.

data.name: Saint Petersburg
data.description: LC Saint Petersburg has been founded in April 2018.

data.name: Sarajevo
data.address:
Zmaja od Bosne bb
71 000 Sarajevo
Bosnia and Herzegovina
data.description: In the summer of 2006, a group of young and motivated students came together, combined their ideas, and focused all their effort into forming EESTEC LC Sarajevo, and with that joined the unique EESTEC family. Noone was surprised when the then small LC Sarajevo grew into a large and powerful organization that gathers a big number of enthusiastic students full of positive energy, eager to meet new people and broaden their horizons. EESTEC Sarajevo is now a mosaic of 150 active and alumni members, and with each new piece, it becomes more diverse. Each individual contributes with their creativity, ideas and knowledge making it possible for LC Sarajevo, as a team, to achieve big things! Our mission is to promote electrical engineering students, and to make it possible for them to travel to major European cities in order to improve their technical knowledge, foreign languages, but also to acquire unique values, lifelong memories, to learn to appreciate different cultures and become richer with every friend they make.

data.name: Skopje
data.address:
Fakultet za Elektrotehnika i informaciski tehnologii
ul. Rugjer Boshkovikj b.b
1000 Skopje
Macedonia
data.description: The Local Committee in Skopje was founded in the year of 2003 and with its 200 members of which more than 90 are active is one of the largest establishments in the EESTEC network. EESTEC LC Skopje for over ten years offers Ss Cyril and Methodius University students’ a new level of complementary education based on improving their soft skills by working on different projects. Also it offers them the chance to upgrade their knowledge by taking part of international projects, learning new things and meeting new people and cultures. Its mission is to expand the students’ views and give them a chance to practically use their skills. Besides that, on international level, LC Skopje also has the task to promote Macedonian culture.

data.name: Tampere
data.address:
TTY/EESTEC
Korkeakoulunkatu 10
33720 Tampere
Finland
data.description: Tampere has been in EESTEC since its very beginning in 1986.The ﬁrst 15 years, it was operated by the Guild of Electrical Engineering, Skilta. At the beginning of 2000 EESTEC LC Tampere became an independent club to serve Computer Science and Electrical Engineering students alike.

data.name: Thessaloniki
data.description: EESTEC LC Thessaloniki was founded in 2015, was promoted to JLC in July 2017 and to LC in November 2017. It is supervised by the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering of the Aristotle University of Thessaloniki (AUTh). Thessaloniki, the northern greek city where the LC is located, is the second largerst city in the country and boasts a rich, complex history, an animated student community and a lively everyday life. Despite being one of the younger LCs, it has already made an impression for its active engagement in EESTEC, participating almost every year in the Soft Skills Academy and EESTech Challenge Projects and successfully organising numerous international events, including International Workshops, Operational Events and International Motivational Weekends. LC Thessaloniki consists of several motivated members full of EESTEC Spirit who study in Thessaloniki, Greece. Μany of them are taking an active role in the International Teams of EESTEC, all of them are eager to learn, travel, make friends and improve themselves through EESTEC. 💖

data.name: Trieste
data.address:
EESTEC LC Trieste - via Fabio Severo, 154 - C.d.S. E3 - 34127 -Trieste
Italy
data.description: EESTEC LC Trieste was born in our city from an idea based on the drive to socialize, to strive for knowledge and internationalization and EESTEC International delivers those values to all its members.Let's travel back in time to the year 2000, when the new millennium started. In the reports of the EESTEC Congress in Delft of that year is hidden our "certificate of birth": Observer Trieste was born on 22nd march 2000 thanks to Danko Tomasic and Marko Nikolic, creating the first core of our group, officially presented by LC Rijeka.The next year the EESTEC Congress in London gave us the rank of "LC" and we officially joined the big family: it was the beginning of a big but not easy adventure.The first years were the most difficult, as may happen to any students' association: we had to create the "brand" of our LC, make us known in the university ambient, to the students (initially just electronic engineering and computer science) and the municipality. All of that while organizing some events, increasing the number of participants on each event, gathering LC members and meeting with other EESTECers in the international area.The year 2006 was a year of change, when a new board decided to insert the organization even more in the academic ambient, creating closer networks with professors, the IEEE local organization and making the basic international part of EESTEC happen. It was the group led by Arling Doberdolani, which in the three years following, brought "Wake up for Bavisela" to life, the first locally organized international event.It was somehow a heroic event for the adaptability, initiative and will of realizing something important with very limited budgets. The shared ideal of getting in contact with other ambients, cultures and making international friends was the driving force for all the organizers.Another big year was 2012 when Erni Durdevic and his very active group were hungry for action and experience, so they performed a burocratic change in the statutes of our association: we switched from a simple student group to a fully legally accepted Cultural Association. Some big workshops on the computer science topic were made and the first really big locally organized international event took place (1st EESTEC Summer School - 2012) with the support of some big regional companies. All those successes were the first big result of years of students believing in our work and in what EESTEC could bring to them.But we didn't stop there, since the next year EESTEC LC Trieste expanded its world by becoming officially an Association of Social Promotion, breaking the record of 15 members, organizing another international technical event (2nd EESTEC Summer School - 2013) and organizing and participating to more local events like the Mini Maker Faire Trieste, the first Arduino workshops and a workshop about the EFT technique to reduce the stress of the exam sessions.The last international event was a big news for us since it was not technical (Game of Teams) but about Soft Skills, the necessary and never taught set of abilities to understand how humans work together and how to do it without creating conflicts and maximizing the team efficiency.That's our story, that's who we are. Now what about you? Would you be part of it too by participating to our next workshop? ;)

data.name: Tuzla
data.address: Franjevacka 2,75000 Tuzla, Bosnia and Herzegovina
data.description: While they were on International Electrical Engineering Students Gathering, widely known as Elektrijada, which took place in Ohrid, in 2006 , Jasmina Nalic and Hajrija Jahic have come across with the idea of establishing EESTEC in Tuzla. Members of LC Rijeka had a  big part in planting that idea. After that, “The Observer Team Tuzla”, which was led by Jasmina Nalic and Marina Beneš, got registered. We became Junior LC during Congress in Budapest 2007, where we were represented by Hajrija Jahic. Members of the ﬁrst board were: Hajrija Jahic-Chairwoman, Mirza Becirbašic - Vice-Chairman, Laris Maglic - Treasurer, Jasmina Nalic - Contact Person, Samir Mešic -Fundraising, Irvin Hot -Web Designer, with the help of our most active members: Marina Beneš, Krešic Igor, Zlatko Alomerovic, Senad Karic, Sandra Hasanovic, Semir Karic, Admir Mujkic, Nadin Dizdarevic, Mirza Nakicevic, Jahic Jasmin , Amela Begic, Amer Bošnjic, Anida Mumic etc. Thanks to their dedication and hard work, EESTEC spirit and ideas have been spread among new generations, and we have become full-ﬂedged local committee. The ﬁrst workshop, organised by LC Tuzla, was held from 3rd to 10th of July, 2007, under the name "Linux as an alternative". This was followed by "GIS & GPS" in 2009, “IPTV-The Next Generation TV” in 2011 and "EESTEChnology" in 2013. Today, after many succesful projects, like 5 of Soft Skills Academies, 4 EESTech Challenges, one AMAZING project that is called EESTEC PROJECT MARATHON which merged 3 big projects together and also a workshop that was held in 2016, EESTEC LC Tuzla counts over a hundred members, and with each day, this number is growing, alongside with EESTEC spirit.

data.name: Xanthi
data.address: Tsimiski 58, Xanthi, 67100, Greece
data.description: LC Xanthi was founded in 2010 by students of the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department of Democritus University of Thrace and became officially a Local Committee in 2014 Congress in Athens. In November 2013, we organized our first international workshop "Rock the Bit". In 2015 we organized our 2nd workshop, "Plan B: Hack the system", as well as our first exchange, "Aquaholics". We also had the chance to organize the HOBOM for 2015, hosting the 2014 and 2015 international boards for 1 week. In March 2016 we organized our 3rd international workshop "Brewing Bad". So far, we are counting more than 80 active members!  We organize many local activities that address not only to the students of our faculty but the whole society of Xanthi, having received excellent feedback! Our goal is to spread the EESTEC spirit, which we accomplish through our highly motivated members and various activities!

data.name: Zagreb
data.address:
FER
Unska 3
10 000 Zagreb
Croatia
data.description:
Established in 2007, EESTEC LC Zagreb has cemented its position at the Faculty of Electrical Engineering and Computing (FER) as a premier student-driven organization. Our primary focus is to connect and empower students at FER as well as other faculties in our city, providing avenues for both personal and professional growth.
We take pride in our track record, consistently recognized through accolades such as the esteemed Rector Awards. These acknowledgements reflect our dedication to maintaining high standards and our continual strive for excellence.
While our workshops aim to deepen participants' academic and practical understanding, we also place a significant emphasis on soft skill development and team dynamics. 
We love to give back to our hardworking members and to our community which is why we proudly organize regular motivational retreats, training sessions, team building and humanitarian fundraising events. That is how we keep the balance between personal and professional growth and manage to foster a supportive and collaborative community.
We have also conducted many International Workshops, Operational Events and IMWs, and look forward to hosting more future events.

data.name: Zurich
data.address:
AMIV an der ETH Zuerich
EESTEC LC Zurich
CAB E37
Universitätsstrasse 6
8092 Zürich
Switzerland
data.description:
HOW TO JOIN: check out eestec.ethz.ch/join or write us at contact@eestec.ethz.ch
EESTEC LC Zurich is part of AMIV, the student association representing mechanical and electrical engineering students at the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology Zurich (ETH Zurich). We also cooperate with VIS, the student association for computer science students at ETH. Hence, our members are mainly electrical engineering, mechanical engineering and computer science students at ETH.
